data.title: RLJ Lodging Trust Maintains Quarterly Dividend at $0.15 a Share, Payable April 15 to Shareholders of Record as of March 31
Banks are feeling the strain as lower-income consumers struggle with inflation and tariff chaos
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi

A JPMorgan analyst breaks down comments from banks about 2025, as financial cushions flatten for lower-end consumers

JPMorgan Chase banking analyst Vivek Juneja said Friday that banks could feel the crunch from U.S. consumers - especially lower-income consumers - as the first quarter of 2025 wraps up in the next couple of weeks, while overall credit strength remains robust for the sector.

"Consumers financial positioning especially at the lower end is being eroded by the impact of cumulative inflation with threat of further erosion with impact of tariffs and policies as well as layoffs," Juneja said in a research note. "The cushion (deposits less spending) is less than prepandemic levels for lower income and/or mass market segment - this will limit their ability to withstand a slowdown."

His summary of bank comments issued in recent weeks provided a glimpse of the economic conditions facing the financial sector as it wraps up the first quarter.

The big banks will issue profit updates, starting on April 11 for JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Wells Fargo & Co. (WFC) and Morgan Stanley (MS); April 14 for Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (GS); and April 15 for Citigroup Inc. (C) and Bank of America Corp. (BAC)

Citing comments on some cracks in the U.S. consumer by Walmart Inc. (WMT) and others in recent weeks, Juneja said spending data has been mixed.

Credit-card spending among Chase customers slowed to about 2.6% growth in February, from 3.7% to 4% between Nov. 1 and Jan. 30, but it's too early to see how those trends continued in March.

Spending has shifted to the wealthier consumers, which remains relatively healthy.

Delinquencies are rising on government student loans, and Federal Housing Administration 30-89 day delinquencies on mortgages are up sharply to 9% - well above the 2012-19 levels of 5% to 7.6%.

Credit-card delinquencies have been " flattish" recently and are highest at U.S. Bancorp (USB), Wells Fargo, and Citi due to those banks' large exposure to private-label cards, he said.

"Delinquencies seem to be flattening and increase in net charge-offs has been slowing but higher tariffs and layoffs could change this," Juneja said.

Overall, bank stocks have sold off about 15% from their recent peak in the face of jitters about an economic slowdown, as well as uncertainty about policies and their implications, he said.

This is all driving a drop in consumer confidence.

"The pendulum had swung sharply in favor of banks post-elections and now has swung very sharply the other way as these sentiments tend to go in extremes," Juneja said. "Clarity on policies and signs of meaningful reduction in cumulative inflation in essentials (food, healthcare, etc.) will be needed to restore consumer confidence, not just couple of additional rate cuts."

Even with the selloff, however, money-center banks are trading at 1.5 times the price to total book value on average. This is "a tad" above the long-term average of 1.3 times, he said.

-Steve Gelsi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: India's Wipro to redraw global business lines
data.description:
Indian IT major Wipro is realigning its four global business lines from April 1 to better address client needs, the company said on Friday.

This is its first rejig after CEO Srinivas Pallia took over the role in April 2024, and comes two years after a similar move under former top boss Thierry Delaporte.

Wipro has brought all cloud, data, analytics and AI, and other related businesses under its tech services business line. Its head of cloud resigned on Friday.

The company's consulting business line will be headed by Amit Kumar, who was hired from Accenture Consulting last month.

"This evolution of our business lines will enable us to further sharpen our focus towards client needs with consulting-led and AI-powered solutions," Pallia said in a statement.

Following the overhaul, Capco - Wipro's largest acquisition - will operate separately under the consulting business line with Capco's head reporting to Pallia. Its Designit business will go under the tech services business line. Both acquired companies were previously under the consulting business.

Apart from the existing engineering business line, the company also announced a fourth newly created line called Business Process Services, with Jasjit Kang as its head.

data.title: Prada's CEO flies to New York as speculation swirls over Versace bid
data.description:
Luxury fashion group Prada's CEO Andrea Guerra has flown to New York, a source close to the matter told Reuters, as the group is in talks to buy Capri Holdings-owned Versace in a deal that would unite two of the biggest names in Italian fashion.

On his trip to the U.S. financial capital, Guerra is accompanied by Lorenzo Bertelli, the son of the group's leading shareholders Miuccia Prada and Patrizio Bertelli, who is chief marketing officer, the source added.

New York based Capri Holding said on Thursday that Donatella Versace was stepping down as main designer for the Versace brand after almost three decades.

From April 1, Dario Vitale, a designer coming from Miu Miu, a smaller brand within the Prada group, will take over Donatella Versace's role as chief creative officer of the Medusa head brand.

The move could facilitate a potential acquisition of Versace by Prada, Morningstar analysts said.

Prada declined to comment. Reuters wasn't able to verify whether Guerra's trip was connected to the talks with Capri Holdings about a potential Versace's deal.

Earlier this month Prada was said to be close to a deal to buy Versace for a price of nearly 1.5 billion euros ($1.64 billion).

On February 20, a source close to the matter told Reuters that Prada had been given four weeks to conduct its assessment of the financial data of its smaller rival.

Prada has also been reported to be interested in buying Jimmy Choo, another Capri brand.

($1 = 0.9168 euros)

data.title: ICE Canada Morning Comment: Facing Another Day Of Losses
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Intercontinental Exchange canola futures were turning lower on Friday morning after coming out of the overnight session with gains.

Over the course of this week, the May canola contract has lost $78.10 per metric tonlargely due to China threatening to slap 100 per cent tariffs on its imports of Canadian canola oil and meal.

Ongoing uncertainty over United States levies added to canola's woes.

The Canadian oilseed was getting support from upticks in Chicago soybeans and soyoil, along with Malaysian palm oil. Canola's increases were tempered by declines in European rapeseed and Chicago soymeal. Small gains in crude oil lent support to the vegetable oils.

The Canadian Grain Commission reported canola exports for the week ended March 9 of 197,200 tons were almost 87 per cent higher than the previous week. That brought year-to-date canola exports to more than 6.32 million tons, compared to around 3.44 million a year ago.

Newly minted federal Liberal leader Mark Carney will be sworn in this morning as Canada's next Prime Minister, with reports stating Dominic LeBlanc is to become the new minister for international trade.

The Canadian dollar was relatively steady on Friday morning, with the loonie at 69.45 U.S. cents, compared to Thursday's close of 69.40.

Approximately 9,550 contracts were traded by 9:38 EDT and prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton were:

 
           Price      Change 
May       566.30     dn 0.60 
Jul       579.20     dn 0.70 
Nov       588.40     dn 1.40 
Jan       597.10     dn 0.50

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: French vintner hopes Trump's tariff threat on European wine is just posturing
data.description:
Key points:
  • Bordeaux vintner fears 200% tariff would end exports to US
  • Hopes that Trump's threat is just a negotiating stance
  • Hopes LVMH chief Bernard Arnault can mollify Trump

The owner of one of France's top Bordeaux wine chateaux hopes U.S. President Donald Trump's threat of 200% tariffs on European wine is just posturing because the impact would be devastating if not.

Trump on Thursday threatened to slap a 200% tariff on European wines and other alcoholic products unless the European Union scraps its planned tax on American whiskey.

"We hope this is just a new chapter of 'The Art of the Deal', because if it is not, we could be ruined," said Florence Cathiard, owner of Chateau Smith Haut Laffite, which employs 60 people and cultivates 80 hectares around Martillac, southwest France.

Referring to Trump's 1987 book, she said she hopes the 200% threat is just a negotiating stance to win the upper hand in a standoff with the European Union.

"Look at what he tells people in his book, he says that you have to strike very, very hard, and then you have to negotiate. So we hope it's just a posture," said Cathiard, whose chateau sells more than 20% of its annual production - about 100,000 bottles of red and 18,000 of white - in the United States.

At an average price of about $100 a bottle, a 200% tariff would push the retail price to $300.

"It will be impossible to sell it if you have to multiply by three," she said, adding that the U.S. threat is another blow after losing Chinese markets and stiff competition from low-wage Latin American producers.

"We have already lost China, three years ago, when China closed itself. So we sent brand ambassadors to Southeast Asia to compensate, and we managed, at high cost. But what can replace the U.S.? Not Russia, you know? We are a little lost," she said.

Cathiard said the French wine industry is pinning its hopes on French luxury goods billionaire Bernard Arnault, Europe's richest man, who attended Trump's inauguration alongside American tech tycoons.

"He knows Donald Trump well. Maybe he will build a factory, or something for bags or fashion in the States. And maybe he will manage for us, because he also has interests in cognac and wine, to escape this awful tax," she said.

EU wine exports to the United States were worth 4.9 billion euros last year, accounting for 29% of the EU's overall exports of wine, according to EU statistics agency Eurostat. Of all EU wine exports to the United States, France made up almost half and Italy almost 40%.

"If Trump makes good on his threat, that will be the end of us. We hope it is just posturing," Cathiard said.

data.title: Skopje bourse's MBI-10 down in week ended Feb 14, turnover grows
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), March 14 (SeeNews) - The benchmark index of the Macedonian Stock Exchange (MSE), MBI-10, fell 3.2% week-on-week to 10,660.65 points on Friday, as total trading turnover on the bourse increased.

Turnover on the official market of the MSE went up to 122.3 million denars ($2.2 million/2 million euro) this week from 119.9 million denars the previous week.

Komercijalna Banka [MSE:KMB] was the most traded share on the stock exchange this week, generating a turnover of 51.1 million denars. However, its shares ended the week at 28,550 denars, down from 28,690 at the end of the previous week.

The OMB bond index stood at 124.48 points on Friday, unchanged compared with the end of the previous week.

Following are the closing share prices of the members of the MBI-10 index (in denars):

data.title: Hub Cyber Security Secures 14-Day Extension to Comply With Nasdaq's Minimum Bid Price Requirement
data.description:
Hub Cyber Security said Friday that the the Nasdaq Hearings Panel has granted its request for a 14-day extension to regain compliance with the bourse's minimum bid price requirement.

The company said it now has until April 14 to achieve a closing bid price of at least $1 per share for 10 consecutive trading days.

data.title: Oil Futures Post Modest Early Gains — Market Talk
data.description: Crude futures are hovering in positive territory as U.S. stocks open higher and the market sees less chance of a quick ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, although prospects of an end to the Russia/Ukraine war are still seen bearish for crude. A peace deal "would likely allow Russia to export oil and natural gas sanction free," Robert Yawger at Mizuho says in a note, adding that Russia would likely produce above its OPEC+ quota if there was demand, and "probably move quickly to start the export of natural gas into the EU." WTI and Brent are up 0.2% at $66.70 and $70.03 a barrel, respectively.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: AtkinsRealis Group Price Target Raised to $92 at RBC
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets raised its price target on AtkinsRealis Group Inc. to $92 from $89 on Friday.

Analyst Sabahat Khan maintained an Outperform rating on shares of the Montreal-based engineering and construction company following its quarterly results.

"AtkinsRealis reported consolidated Q4 results that were short of expectations (good results from Engineering + Nuclear more than offset by a larger-than-expected LSTK loss), while 2025 guidance was in line with expectations and reflects another year of solid top-line growth and margin expansion in the 2 core segments," Khan said in a note to clients.

"The solid 2025 guide and a clean pro-forma balance sheet positions the company well for the year ahead," the analyst said.

data.title: Science Applications International Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.37 a Share, Payable April 25 to Holders as of April 11
Tensions Rise Over Apple's Encryption Policy as U.K. and U.S. Hold High-Level Talks
data.description:
U.K. and U.S. officials have held private talks to address concerns that Britain is pressuring Apple to create a back door into encrypted user data, Bloomberg News reported. British officials claim they are not seeking broad access but want to correct what they call a misleading narrative.

The discussions follow Apple's decision to disable end-to-end encryption for cloud data in the U.K., complying with a government order. The move has sparked backlash, with U.S. Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard calling it an egregious violation of American rights.

Apple is challenging the order, with a closed-door hearing set for Friday at London's High Court. U.K. officials insist data requests would be limited to serious crimes like terrorism and child exploitation, requiring separate warrants and primarily targeting U.K. individuals, not U.S. citizens.

Meanwhile, human rights groups Liberty and Privacy International have joined Apple in opposing the directive, calling it a threat to global security. A bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers has also pushed for the U.K. court to remove a gag order they argue stifles free speech and limits congressional oversight.

Despite the dispute, British officials stress that intelligence cooperation between the two countries remains strong and will continue under the new U.S. administration.

data.title: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Retains Quarterly Dividend at $0.25 a Share, Payable April 15 to Holders of Record as of March 31
Jampack Elevates Security Standards in Event Travel Packages, Now SOC 2 Type 1 Certified
data.description:
Certification Demonstrates Commitment to Data Security, Risk Management, and Operational Excellence in the Rapidly Growing Travel Technology Sector

Las Vegas, Nevada--(Newsfile Corp. - March 14, 2025) - Jampack, the leading platform in ticket and hotel packages for live events, has successfully obtained its Service Organization Control (SOC) 2 Type 1 certification. This achievement validates Jampack's adherence to industry standards, reinforcing its commitment to maintaining a secure and reliable infrastructure for its enterprise clients.

Jampack, the leading platform specializing in ticket and hotel packages

"Earning SOC 2 Type 1 certification demonstrates Jampack's continued dedication to maintaining rigorous security and operational excellence," said Andrew Citores, CEO of Jampack. "As the company connects event producers, tour operators, and integrated resorts with travelers, maintaining trust and protecting data are essential. This certification provides our partners and their customers with assurance that sensitive information is safeguarded at an enterprise-grade level."

Importance of SOC 2 Certification

Trust and security are non-negotiable for a platform servicing event travel bookings for hundreds of thousands of customers and safeguarding their personal information. The SOC 2 Type 1 certification represents an independent verification that Jampack has implemented appropriate controls to safeguard data within the platform.

For Jampack customers and partners, this certification signifies:

  • Validated security measures

  • Enhanced trust and reliability

  • Reduced counterparty risk for our partners

  • Enterprise-grade infrastructure

Strengthening the Travel Technology Ecosystem

The travel industry is increasingly relying on technology partners to create seamless booking experiences. When selecting these partners, companies naturally consider the security risks involved. The SOC 2 certification provides assurance that:

  • Jampack has designed appropriate security controls
  • Jampack's platform meets the needs of enterprise-level operations Customer data is protected throughout the booking journey

This certification is particularly valuable for companies who need to ensure they're working with reliable, secure technology providers as they expand their digital offerings.

About Jampack's Platform

Jampack is the leading platform for creating comprehensive travel packages, seamlessly bundling event tickets, hotel accommodations, exclusive experiences, and perks. Serving over 180,000 attendees across more than 400 events, Jampack empowers event producers, tour operators, and integrated resorts to increase revenue, simplify operations, and elevate guest experiences-all without the need to manage complex technology in-house.

To learn more about how Jampack can streamline your event offerings and boost attendee satisfaction, visit www.jampack.com.

SOC 2 Type 1 Badge

Press inquiries

Jampack

https://www.jampack.com

William Brooks

press@jampack.com

7165 Rafael Ridge Way, Las Vegas, NV 89119

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/244484

data.title: REG - CRH PLC - Preliminary Proxy Statement & Notice of AGM
data.description:
RNS Number : 7877A CRH PLC 14 March 2025  

March 14, 2025

CRH plc (the "Company")

Re: Preliminary Proxy Statement and Irish Statutory Accounts

Today, the Company filed a Preliminary Proxy Statement for the 2025 Annual General Meeting of the Company on Schedule 14A with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC").

The Preliminary Proxy Statement is filed with the SEC in accordance with the U.S. Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The Preliminary Proxy Statement is available to view on the SEC's website at https://www.sec.gov and the Company's website at https://www.crh.com.

The financial statements of the Company for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as adopted by the European Union (the "Irish Statutory Accounts") were published by the Company today and are also available to view on the Company's website at: https://www.crh.com.

The Preliminary Proxy Statement and the Irish Statutory Accounts have also been submitted to the U.K. National Storage Mechanism and will shortly be available for inspection at: https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism.

Enquiries

Company Secretary

data.title: Canada's Carney Is an Economic Heavyweight. That May Not Matter in a Trump Trade Fight. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Brian Swint

The next prime minister of Canada is in many ways the polar opposite of U.S. President Donald Trump. That may give him an advantage — or end up working against him.

Mark Carney is so many things Donald Trump isn't — academic, cosmopolitan, and from a middle-class background in the remote West of the country. After a career in finance and stints as governor of both the Bank of Canada and the Bank of England — the only person to ever lead two Group of Seven central banks — he is being sworn in as Justin Trudeau's replacement on Friday after winning the contest to become Liberal Party leader.

The question is whether he has what it takes to make Canada thrive as Trump tries to turn it into the 51st state. Carney likes to say trade wars are like ice hockey — Canada will win.

The incoming prime minister is "someone who ultimately cares about people, about improving outcomes for people — and that makes him a good economist and a well-grounded leader," said Silvana Tenreyro, an economics professor at the London School of Economics who worked with Carney on the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee. "Mark was a stellar governor at the BOE, knowledgeable, hard-working and invariably driven by the Bank's mandate and mission."

Given his expertise in economics — a PhD from Oxford University is the least of his credentials — Carney would seem extremely well placed to outmaneuver Trump when it comes to maximizing prosperity for the nation. But history suggests that what works in economics doesn't always work in politics.

It is extremely rare for a central banker to go on to become a nation's political leader. When they have, they don't have a great record.

The most recent high-profile example is Mario Draghi, the former president of the European Central Bank who was invited to serve as Italy's prime minister when squabbling parties left the country's government in gridlock during the Covid-19 pandemic. He managed to push through an economic stimulus package, but he was forced to resign after less than two years.

Draghi never had to face voters in an election, and he was succeeded by Giorgia Meloni, a right-wing politician in the same vein as Trump. That suggests whatever technical expertise Draghi brought to the job wasn't particularly appreciated by the citizenry.

Another example is Manmohan Singh, India's prime minister from 2004 to 2014. He was praised for overseeing critical economic reforms as India integrated into world markets. He was also — unlike Mario Draghi — able to win elections. At the same time, with his quiet and composed demeanor, he was criticized in his second term for being indecisive as political scandals among his ministers arose, and his approval rating sank in his second term. Singh died at age 92 in December.

Central bankers are known for making difficult decisions, and raising interest rates to contain inflation is often unpopular. Yet there are certainly areas where politicians and monetary policymakers have overlapping skill sets — both have to build consensus and coalitions, and be the public face of national institutions.

Carney was arguably the political leader of the U.K. in 2016 — at least temporarily. In the hours after Britain voted to leave the European Union, Prime Minister David Cameron resigned, creating a political vacuum. The pound tanked. The nation looked to Carney for guidance, and he was able to calm nerves by saying the BOE would protect the financial system.

Carney's other claim to fame is navigating Canada, as BOC governor, through the 2008-09 financial crisis without falling into recession. It was the only G-7 country to manage that.

The biggest challenge might be selling his economic know-how to the people, as even those with the greatest knowledge don't always make the best connection with voters. As former European Commission President Jean-Claude Juncker once said: "We all know what to do, but we don't know how to get re-elected once we have done it."

Another problem is that Carney still represents the old world order that many people feel has left them behind. Frustration with the textbook way of doing government policy is what pushed many U.S. voters to Trump and, arguably, away from Canada's Trudeau, who stepped aside after his own ministers said they lost confidence in his policies.

Carney hasn't yet faced a popular vote and his tenure as prime minister may not last long. A federal election must be called this year, and he's expected to do it quickly. A vote has to come by Oct. 20 at the latest. Opposition leader Pierre Poilievre leads in opinion polls, but not by much. Carney has narrowed the gap to 1 point in opinion polls from almost 20 points a month ago.

So, despite central bankers' patchy record in politics, it looks like a lot of Canadians will be willing to take a chance on one in the face of a new economic crisis.

Write to Brian Swint at brian.swint@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: TSX Trims Weekly Losses
data.description:
The S&P/TSX Composite Index rose 0.5% to above the 24,300 level on Friday, trimming some of the weekly losses of over 1.5% and rebounding from four-month lows as positive economic data and the prospect of a policy turnaround drive optimism.

Manufacturing sales increased by 3% year-on-year in January and wholesale sales rose by 2.8% over the same period, signaling economic resilience amid persistent U.S. trade tensions.

Furthermore, the forthcoming swearing-in of former central banker Mark Carney as prime minister, who is expected to counteract U.S. tariff threats on Canadian goods, has added further support.

The recovery was led by tech and financial heavyweights, with e-commerce leader Shopify adding nearly 3% to recover from sharp losses, while Constellation Software and Celestica contributed additional gains of over 1% and 3% respectively.

Additionally, major banks such as RBC, TD Bank, and BMO each advanced more than 0.7%.

data.title: CT REIT Declares March Dividend
data.description:
CT Real Estate Investment Trust (CRT-UN.TO) on Friday said it will pay a March dividend of $0.07710 per trust unit, payable on April 15 to unitholders of record on March 31.

The March dividend is unchanged from the prior month.

data.title: Travis Perkins Shares Fall on 2024 Earnings Delay
data.description:
(13:29 GMT) *Travis Perkins Backs 2024 View
(13:46 GMT) Travis Perkins Shares Fall on 2024 Earnings Delay

By Ian Walker

Travis Perkins shares fell after the company said that its 2024 earnings would be delayed as its auditor needs more time to complete standard procedures, and backed its guidance.

Shares were down 59.50 pence, or 10%, at 512.0 pence in early afternoon European trading. They are currently down 30% over the year to date.

The FTSE 250-listed builders' merchant and home improvement retailer group said Friday that it would publish earnings for the year ended Dec. 31 as soon as possible. They were originally due to be reported on March 18.

Travis Perkins said it expects adjusted operating profit--which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--for the year to be in line with guidance provided on Oct. 24. At that time the company cut its guidance for the metric to 135 million pounds ($174.8 million), from 150 million pounds previously forecast, which it blamed on a shortfall in the merchanting segment

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1330 GMT on Friday:

** Liberty Global < LBTYA.O > has approached Britain's Vodafone < VOD.L > for a possible acquisition of its stake in their Dutch joint venture VodafoneZiggo, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

** Recycled metal products maker Radius Recycling RDUS.O said on Thursday it had entered into a definitive merger agreement with Toyota Tsusho America, a U.S. subsidiary of Japanese trading house Toyota Tsusho Corp (TTC) 8015.T.

** Home brokerage Compass COMP.N is in advanced talks to acquire Warren Buffett's real-estate brokerage business, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: AeroVironment Says Army Cancels February Stop-Work Order
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

AeroVironment said Friday that the U.S. defense department has lifted the stop-work order issued last month.

The Department of the Army in February issued a stop-work order on certain existing U.S. government contracts, which included foreign military sales funded by the U.S. government by way of foreign military financing.

AeroVironment, a defense contractor, said that it is assessing the effect of the delay caused by the issuance of the stop-work order and the timing of its fulfilment of its obligations under the applicable contracts.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Sterling Receives Comptroller of the Currency Approval for Sale of Sterling Bank to EverBank
data.description:
Sterling Bancorp said Friday it has received approval from the Comptroller of the Currency to sell all of the issued and outstanding shares of its Sterling Bank and Trust unit to EverBank Financial.

Sterling said it will receive a fixed purchase price of $261 million in cash in the sale and that it intends to complete the transaction following the expiration of all applicable waiting periods.

The deal was first announced in September.

data.title: REG - Brunner Investment - Net Asset Value(s)
data.description:
RNS Number : 7885A Brunner Investment Trust PLC 14 March 2025  

The Brunner Investment Trust PLC

LEI: 529900S0Y9ZINCHB3O93

As recommended by the AIC, net asset values are calculated on both a capital and a cum-income basis.

The Brunner Investment Trust PLC announces that at close of business 13 March 2025:

1)  based on the par value of the company's long term debt and preference shares, the capital net asset value per ordinary share was 1350.76p.

2)  based on the market value of the company's long term debt and preference shares, the capital net asset value per ordinary share was 1373.44p.

3)  based on the par value of the company's long term debt and preference shares, the cum-income net asset value per ordinary share was 1357.18p.

4)  based on the market value of the company's long term debt and preference shares, the cum-income net asset value per ordinary share was 1379.85p.

In the valuation of the company's long term debt at market value, the margin added to the yield of the relevant reference gilt is derived from the spread of BBB UK corporate bond yields over gilt yields.

Enquiries:

Kirsten Salt

Tel: 020 3246 7513

14 March 2025
